sourceMovie.title: 28 Years Later
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: 28 Years Later works as a horror movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The horror and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: World War Z
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: World War Z (2013) is the kind of film 28 Years Later fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The horror overlap is real, but it's the dark emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

recommendations.title: 10 Cloverfield Lane
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: 10 Cloverfield Lane shares 28 Years Later's gift for horror — the 2016 film delivers thriller without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

recommendations.title: 28 Days Later
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Much of what makes 28 Years Later compelling — the horror, the thriller — shows up in 28 Days Later (2002) in a slightly different form. Both films understand that dark storytelling works best when it trusts the audience.

recommendations.title: 28 Weeks Later
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The horror energy of 28 Years Later is alive in 28 Weeks Later (2007), filtered through a different story but the same emotional register. It earns its place on this list with a dark payoff that rivals the original pick.

recommendations.title: Land of the Dead
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Land of the Dead (2005) operates in the same horror as 28 Years Later, but with its own distinct voice and pacing. The dark payoff will feel satisfyingly familiar without retreading the same ground.

recommendations.title: Bird Box
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Bird Box (2018) belongs to the same conversation as 28 Years Later — both anchor their horror in thriller emotion that doesn't let go. The dark undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

recommendations.title: War of the Worlds
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If 28 Years Later's thriller stayed with you, War of the Worlds (2005) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The science-fiction through-line is the real common thread between them.

recommendations.title: Twelve Monkeys
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where 28 Years Later excels in thriller, Twelve Monkeys (1995) doubles down on science-fiction to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: Predator
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Predator (1987) is the natural next step after 28 Years Later: same thriller DNA, different perspective. The tense streak and science-fiction elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: The Purge: Election Year
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of 28 Years Later who respond to its horror will find The Purge: Election Year (2016) immediately familiar. Both films share a thriller sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

faq.question: What should I watch after 28 Years Later?
faq.answer: Try World War Z, 10 Cloverfield Lane, 28 Days Later—each shares DNA with 28 Years Later while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is 28 Years Later?
faq.answer: 28 Years Later (2025) is best described as Horror, Thriller, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is 28 Years Later part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "28 Days/Weeks/Years Later Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
